source_item.title: The New Oil? Why the world is chasing copper

slide1_hook.label: Copper Is the New Oil. Here's Why.
slide1_hook.subhead: The metal powering EVs and AI is running out.

slide2_backstory.label: THE BACKSTORY
slide2_backstory.headline: Copper Was Always Important. Now It's Critical.
slide2_backstory.body: Copper has long been a boring but essential metal for wires and electronics. Now EVs, solar, wind, and AI data centers all need far more copper than before, straining supply.

slide3_news.label: THE UPDATE
slide3_news.headline: Demand Is Surging. Supply Cannot Keep Up.
slide3_news.body: The world could face a 30% copper supply shortfall by 2035, the IEA says. Mines are getting harder to find, so Goldman Sachs sees prices near $15,000 a tonne by 2035, up from about $10,000-13,000 now.

slide4_why.label: WHY IT MATTERS
slide4_why.headline: No Copper Means No Clean Energy
bullets: India's 500 GW clean energy goal needs copper it lacks
AI data centers alone may need 550,000 tonnes by 2030
Copper is now a national security priority worldwide

slide5_engage.label: YOUR TAKE
slide5_engage.headline: India imports over half its copper but has untapped reserves at home. Should we mine them, even with environmental costs?
slide5_engage.cta: Follow for more stories on the commodities shaping India's future 🔩
